SAIF SURGICAL & SCIENTIFIC EQUIPMENT — Images Folder
=====================================================

All required images are INCLUDED in this folder.
You do NOT need to source or rename any images — just use the export as-is.

INCLUDED IMAGE FILES:
---------------------
logo.png                  — Business logo (professional medical logo, transparent background)
hero-medical.jpg          — Homepage hero background (hospital/OT room, 1920x1080)
category-surgical.jpg     — Surgical Instruments category card (600x400)
category-laboratory.jpg   — Laboratory Equipment category card (600x400)
category-scientific.jpg   — Scientific Tools category card (600x400)
category-educational.jpg  — Educational Equipment category card (600x400)
blog-ot-setup.jpg         — Blog post image: OT setup article (800x500)
blog-equipment.jpg        — Blog post image: New equipment article (800x500)
blog-tips.jpg             — Blog post image: Buying tips article (800x500)

USAGE:
------
- All images are already referenced correctly in index.html
- The hero background is set in style.css: background-image: url('images/hero-medical.jpg')
- To replace an image, just overwrite the file with the same filename
- Use JPG for photos, PNG for logos/graphics
- Keep file sizes under 500KB each for fast loading

FOLDER STRUCTURE:
-----------------
  saif-surgical/
  ├── index.html
  ├── style.css
  ├── script.js
  └── images/
      ├── logo.png                  (INCLUDED)
      ├── hero-medical.jpg          (INCLUDED)
      ├── category-surgical.jpg     (INCLUDED)
      ├── category-laboratory.jpg   (INCLUDED)
      ├── category-scientific.jpg   (INCLUDED)
      ├── category-educational.jpg  (INCLUDED)
      ├── blog-ot-setup.jpg         (INCLUDED)
      ├── blog-equipment.jpg        (INCLUDED)
      └── blog-tips.jpg             (INCLUDED)

DEPLOYMENT:
-----------
Upload the entire export folder (index.html + style.css + script.js + images/) to:
- GitHub Pages: push to a repo, enable Pages in Settings > Pages
- Netlify: drag & drop the folder at netlify.com/drop
- Any web host: upload via FTP to your public_html folder
